title: CSS animation generation — CSS springs | Motion
insights: Motion's CSS animation generator produces optimized keyframes for use without the library. Converts spring physics to @keyframes + linear() CSS. Enables Motion-designed animations in CSS-only contexts or as static export.

title: View Transitions API (single-document) | Can I use
insights: Chrome 111+, Edge 111+, Firefox 133+, Safari 18+. Firefox 144 makes it Baseline Newly Available (Oct 2025). Cross-document (MPA) transitions: Chrome 126+, others pending. Same-document universally safe in late 2025.

title: Animating View Transitions — patterns.dev
insights: Patterns.dev guide on View Transitions. Custom CSS animations via ::view-transition-old and ::view-transition-new pseudo-elements. view-transition-name for named transitions. Cross-document with @view-transition rule. Practical SPA patterns with React Router and Next.js.

title: Ten tips for better CSS transitions and animations — Josh Collinsworth
insights: 10 tips: (1) don't animate layout properties, (2) use animation-fill-mode, (3) stagger with delay, (4) use custom properties for dynamic transitions, (5) respect prefers-reduced-motion, (6) don't transition everything on hover, (7) use will-change sparingly, (8) watch for transition conflicts.

title: Investigate Animation Performance with Chrome DevTools | Calibre
insights: DevTools: Performance tab → frame rate chart → green = composite (fast), yellow = JS, purple = layout. Layers panel shows promoted GPU layers. will-change visible in Rendering panel. Identifies jank: layout thrashing, forced reflows, paint storms.

title: Updates in hardware-accelerated animation capabilities | Chrome
insights: Chrome automatically hardware-accelerates transform and opacity animations. Independent transform properties (translate, scale, rotate) now run on compositor separately. clip-path animations increasingly compositor-eligible. Hardware scroll animations via useScroll/scroll().

title: 60 FPS Performant Web Animations — Algolia Engineering
insights: 16.7ms frame budget. Composite-only properties (transform, opacity) = paint-free. Layout thrashing avoidance: batch DOM reads before writes. requestAnimationFrame synchronization. Layer promotion strategy vs memory cost. Practical optimization checklist.

title: CSS Scroll-Driven Animations Guide — design.dev
insights: Complete guide: scroll-timeline CSS property, animation-timeline: scroll(root) / scroll(nearest). @scroll-timeline vs CSS function syntax. view() animation for element entrance on scroll. Named timelines for multiple animations sharing one scroll container.

title: Motion Design System — A Practical Guide | Medium
insights: Atomic approach: atoms (fade, slide, scale), molecules (modal enter = fade+scale), organisms (page transition = slide+stagger). Document animation vocabulary before implementation. Handoff: motion specs with easing graphs, duration values, timing diagrams.

title: Working Together: UX Motion Specs for Design and Development — IBM Design
insights: IBM motion spec process: designers create annotated motion specs with easing curves, duration values, AfterEffects previews. Developers implement from spec using carbon-motion package. Shared vocabulary: productive vs expressive, entrance vs standard vs exit easing.

title: Creating Skeleton UI Loading Animation — ProtoPie
insights: Skeleton animation techniques in ProtoPie: gradient mask moving left-to-right (shimmer). Key: 200-500ms timing, match content layout exactly, transition skeleton→content with fade (no flicker). Shows how to prototype skeleton→content transition for handoff.

title: 12 Micro Animation Examples Bringing Apps to Life in 2025
insights: 2025 micro-interaction catalog: button state confirmations (200ms), form validation feedback, notification badge ping, loading skeleton shimmer, toggle switch spring, pull-to-refresh physics, like/heart particle burst, typing indicator bounce. 120-220ms optimal for micro-animations.

title: Micro Interactions That Feel Magical — netcodesign
insights: Pattern library: hover lift (translateY -2px, box-shadow), click ripple (scale 0→1 from click point), success checkmark draw (SVG stroke-dashoffset), loading dots (bounce stagger 150ms offset), input focus glow (box-shadow spring). All under 300ms.

title: Disney's Motion Principles in Designing Interface Animations | Marvel
insights: Anticipation: pre-animation cue before major action (button slight press before full action). Exaggeration: intentional overstatement for clarity (error shake). Staging: use motion to direct attention hierarchy during complex transitions. Appeal: personality through natural timing.

title: A complete guide to CSS Scroll-driven Animations | Mariana Beldi
insights: Comprehensive CSS scroll-driven guide. animation-range: entry/exit/contain/cover. view() with inset for viewport margins. Multiple simultaneous scroll-driven animations. Interaction with reduced-motion via @media prefers-reduced-motion.

title: Changelog — Motion (formerly Framer Motion)
insights: Motion v12.38 (March 2026): oklch/oklab/lab/lch color-mix animation, hardware-accelerated scroll, layoutAnchor prop, axis-locked layout (layout='x'/'y'), axis-locked drag snapping, skipInitialAnimation for useSpring. 290+ examples documented.

title: Rive: Animate Web UIs with State Machines — Marmelab
insights: Practical Rive state machine integration in React. @rive-app/react-canvas package. useRive hook for state machine inputs. Boolean inputs toggle states, number inputs for progress, triggers for one-shot events. Practical loading indicator and interactive button examples.

title: Integrating Rive into a React Project — Codrops 2025
insights: Production Rive integration case study. Artboard management for multiple animation scenes. State machine inputs via React state. Performance: lazy load runtimes, use canvas renderer. Data binding in v2 for reactive values. GPU renderer vs SVG for complex scenes.

title: Motion — hover.to Design System
insights: Small design system with documented motion token structure: instant (100ms), fast (160ms), base (240ms), slow (360ms). Spring tokens with stiffness/damping variants. Example of real-world motion token tier implementation.

title: Physics-based animations spring to life — Nordcraft Blog
insights: Visual builder approach to spring physics. Real-time spring parameter adjustment. Explains how non-physicists can tune animations: start with duration, add bounce, observe natural feel. Aligns with Apple/Figma's designer-friendly parameter approach.

title: Intro to the Web Animations API — Pawel Grzybek
insights: Practical WAAPI introduction. element.animate() syntax. KeyframeEffect constructor. Animation.play()/pause()/reverse(). getAnimations() for inspection. Performance: WAAPI runs on compositor for transform/opacity. Comparison with CSS transitions and requestAnimationFrame approach.

title: 5 steps for including motion design in your design system — designsystems.com
insights: Step 1: decide scope (micro vs macro). Step 2: establish motion principles aligned to brand. Step 3: define primitive building blocks (fade-in, slide-right, scale-up). Step 4: choreography patterns from primitives. Step 5: dev handoff with annotation tools, motion specs, duration calculators.
